title: how to deal with the highs and lows of youtube (i show my earnings)

transcript.text: Hey guys, today I want to talk about how to deal with the highs and lows of YouTube shorts because I think a lot of you guys have the wrong mindset when you're going into this business. And yeah, let's just call it what it is because it is a business, right? You need to understand this concept if you ever want to have consistent success with YouTube shorts because honestly, I know it sounds cliche, but if your mindset isn't right, there's really no point raking in hell of views. But I'm going to go through examples of some of my earnings and where my mindset was at different points in my journey. So, give me a few minutes to make my points here because I'm going to be giving you insight into my life and how I used to think, which is just why I know for a fact that the mindset really is everything. And by the way, even me, guys, I used to not think mindset was as big of a factor as it is. But the longer you do this and the longer you're in the game, the more you realize that your success completely hinges on your mindset. And if you can develop an unbeatable mindset, man, then success is just inevitable. It's only a matter of time. Because for a lot of you guys, you are motivated by the outcomes of certain things. How much money you're making, how many views or subscribers you're getting. If that's you and you're waking up every day checking your YouTube analytics, bros, you're just going about it all wrong. Now, I get it. I get it. I'm addicted to checking my YouTube studio, too, to be completely honest, right? But for a lot of people when they check their YouTube studio results, especially if they're new, they get discouraged and understandably so, right? Like you uploaded a video and it didn't get views. Especially if you worked hard on that video, it feels like you kind of worked hard for nothing. I love analytics and stats and analytics are great for one reason and one reason only when it comes to YouTube, and the reason is that you can learn from them. That's the only reason why you should be relentlessly checking your stats to be honest, right? Besides basic monitoring and making sure your channel's doing okay, like if you're noticing your videos aren't getting pushed or aren't getting views, instead of being discouraged, you should be curious. What's the swipe rate? How does the graph look? How does the retention look? How can I improve these things? And more importantly, these are much more productive things to think about than thinking about like for example when you should call it quits. A lot of you guys have these self-limiting beliefs. Why would you believe in anything other than something that benefits you? For me, I want the most productive mindset that will make me successful. And successful people don't sit around asking questions like, "When should I give up?" I've seen a lot of you guys ask that question. People who make it, they just figure out a way to either one, make it work, either through sheer willpower or two, through strategy, like for example, changing your strategy. Even if you're asking questions like when to know when to give up, you're cooked. The answer is just right now, bro. Give up. Because the truth is, if you have one foot in the door and one foot out of it, you're not fully invested in your success. That's the truth. And I'm going to get into my analytics in a second here, but I want you guys to really listen to this. I know this might sound crazy to some of you guys, but I'm serious. You guys should just try this for a few months and see for yourselves. See if it helps you out. Just make this one simple change. Take full responsibility and accountability for everything in your life. I mean, everything. Even things that aren't your fault. I'm serious. That's what I did. So, for example, if your video gets zero views, it's your fault. Stop blaming YouTube's algorithm and just do everything within your power to achieve your goals. Instead of whining or complaining about the zero views, just find the solution. There's always a solution to everything. And sure, the solution for you might be harder. It might require more steps. It might be more painstaking than what other people have to go through. That's just life, guys. Life is unfair. I know it's easier said than done. But by doing this, you're going to build up autonomy. You're going to show yourself that you can actually make a change in your own life. Because guess what? If you're responsible for everything, that means you can also take accountability for everything that comes your way, which means the bad stuff, yes, but it also means the good stuff, your successes. If something good happens in your life, it's because of something that you did. You're not just somebody who exists who is just completely swayed around by the environment you're in. Okay? Hopefully not at least. It's your job to go and shape the environment that you want to be in. Now, I want to talk about my own journey and what I was going through at different stages when I earned money. Give you guys my mindset because this is important. Motivation doesn't last forever, right? You know, you need to be disciplined. So, I think it'll actually be pretty helpful for some of you guys to hear from me, my own journey, and what I thought and how I stay consistent to this day. Okay, so this is my main channel. As you can see, in my total lifetime of this channel, I made about almost 20,000. I'm going to hit 20,000 pretty easily. In fact, when I'm recording this, this is the 7th of June. And uh I don't know if you guys can see it. It's in the bottom left corner, but I've already made almost $1,000. Now, like I always tell you guys, I uploaded for a year straight daily. I did not miss a day. Probably like one or two days for like I think I missed probably like one or two days for like surgeries and stuff, bro. But literally a year straight. So anyways, I'm going to go through here, right? I'm just going to scrub through on my arrow keys. I'm showing you guys I didn't make anything for all of this, right? So finally, anyways, around November 20th, I really started to get monetized, right? And then I started to actually make some money off YouTube. It's my first time ever. It was the most sick thing ever, dude. I was like at my deadend retail job. It was late at night and then the first time it ever updated telling me I got some revenue and it was like $5, but I was so hyped, dude. And then it started to get like $10 and I was like, "Oh my god." Right? But it kind of hovered around here, right? Like $10, $20.
